version_note: v1 was owner-re-signed 2026-07-24, then substantively changed (mandatory receipt pair, anon-id binding, output atomicity/alias rules). Those changes required a NEW freeze; the owner re-signed v2 on 2026-07-25, so frozen=true. Parse validity (runner) is separate from Gate-2 authorization; frozen=true is necessary but not sufficient — Gate 2 still needs the resource preflight and a separate owner start command.
principle: Remove identity/assignment labels ONLY. Never delete substantive evidence (code diff, tests, validator output, completion claim). If content itself reveals treatment, flag blinding_compromised; do not sanitize.

acceptable_handoff.rule: a scorer may accept a handoff ONLY when all three exist: the redacted packet, the anonymized receipt, AND the completeness marker (<receipt-out>.handoff-complete), and the marker's packet_sha256 / receipt_sha256 match the two files.
acceptable_handoff.path_aliasing: the runner rejects any --out/--receipt-out that alias each other or any input path (this closed a fail-open where --out == --receipt-out left only the receipt).
acceptable_handoff.atomicity: both payloads are computed before any write; outputs are staged to temp files, replaced into place, then the marker is written; on ANY failure every partial output (packet, receipt, marker, temps) is removed.

producer_output_artifact.name: raw-output.txt
fixed_section_order: 1. FIX_DIFF: git diff of the fix (verbatim)

3. VALIDATOR_OUTPUT: the POST-HOC UNIFORM validator output — the pinned validators run identically on ALL four arms by the scorer/experimenter after outputs are committed. Present for every arm, so it does NOT signal treatment (verbatim).
4. COMPLETION_CLAIM: the producer's prose completion claim
producer_output_artifact.note: Sections 1-3 are substantive evidence and are NEVER redacted. Redaction applies only to section 4 free text and to the receipt metadata (the arm field is dropped).

validator_output_split.reason: WARNING 2: if only Arm D's TREATMENT-TIME validator feedback appeared in the scorer packet, the scorer could infer Arm D. Split the two roles.
validator_output_split.treatment_time_validator_feedback: Arm D input only; kept in EXPERIMENTER PROVENANCE, never placed in raw-output.txt or the scorer packet.
validator_output_split.post_hoc_uniform_validator_output: run identically on all four arms after commit; this is the VALIDATOR_OUTPUT section the scorer sees; uniform, non-signalling.
validator_output_split.residual_leak_rule: if a COMPLETION_CLAIM's substance shows the producer acted on treatment-time validator feedback in a way only Arm D could, set blinding_compromised (do not delete).

canonical_runner.invocation: python redaction_runner.py --contract scorer-handoff-contract.json --raw raw-output.txt --out redacted-packet.json --receipt producer-receipt.json --receipt-out redacted-receipt.json
canonical_runner.fail_closed: the receipt pair (--receipt and --receipt-out) is REQUIRED; the runner will not produce a redacted packet without also anonymizing the receipt. Missing either exits non-zero and writes nothing.
canonical_runner.anonymized_receipt_output: redacted-receipt.json — has the arm field dropped, string metadata redacted via the literal map, and anon_id bound to the redacted packet's anon_id (OUT-<12hex> of raw-output.txt).
canonical_runner.atomicity: both payloads are computed before any write; a rejected input leaves NO output, and if the receipt write fails the already-written packet is removed (no half set).
canonical_runner.redacts_only: the COMPLETION_CLAIM section of raw-output.txt + producer receipt string metadata; FIX_DIFF/TEST_LOG/VALIDATOR_OUTPUT are copied verbatim
canonical_runner.answer_safe: operates only on a producer output; never reads the Gate 0 analysis, the fix, or the answer

fields.contract_sha256: sha256 of this contract file (pins which redaction map ran)
fields.raw_output_sha256: sha256 of the raw-output.txt (binds raw -> redacted)
fields.redacted_output_sha256: sha256 of the emitted redacted text
fields.per_rule_match_count: count of replacements per redaction rule (audit of what was removed)
fields.blinding_compromised: boolean | null (set by experimenter, never by the runner)

anonymous_id.canonical_input_bytes: the UTF-8 bytes of raw-output.txt exactly as submitted (LF, no trailing normalization)
anonymous_id.id_format: OUT- + first 12 lowercase hex chars of sha256(canonical_input_bytes)
anonymous_id.collision_rule: on a 12-hex collision, extend both ids to 16 hex; record the extension
anonymous_id.raw_output_sha256_recorded: the full 64-hex sha256 is stored in the handoff log, binding raw -> redacted and making de-anonymization tamper-evident

redaction.matching: case-insensitive; each pattern is a regex applied globally to the target text in listed order; internal spaces in multi-word patterns match one-or-more whitespace (\s+); filename patterns match the basename regardless of / or \ separator
redaction.determinism: the map is frozen here; running it twice on the same input yields byte-identical redacted output; anyone can replay it.

blinding_compromised.set_by: experimenter, during redaction prep, per output
blinding_compromised.when: a substantive, non-label feature unavoidably signals the treatment (e.g. an output contains validator-feedback text that only the validator-treatment arm receives)
blinding_compromised.rule: record it; do NOT delete the revealing substantive evidence. A compromised-blinding output is still scored, and flagged.

scorer_blinding_check.when: each scorer records this BEFORE the anon-ID -> arm mapping is released

fields_per_output.suspected_treatment: one of A | B | C | D | unsure
fields_per_output.reason: free text — what in the redacted output drove the guess

scorer_blinding_check.purpose: measures whether blinding held; a high-confidence correct guess rate near chance supports blinding, well above chance indicates leakage to reconcile with blinding_compromised flags.

roles.producers: four ANSWER-BLIND contexts: they do not know the root cause or fix, but each knows which treatment it received (Arm B knows it has the Skill packet, etc.). Answer-blind, not treatment-blind.
roles.scorers: two ARM-IDENTITY-BLIND contexts: before scoring they do not know the A/B/C/D mapping for any anon_id.
roles.experimenter: may know the answer; runs redaction and holds the mapping; is neither a producer nor a scorer.

release_gate: the anon-ID -> arm mapping is released only after both scorers submit their scores AND their blinding-check guesses.
